content: I will now replace the HTML `<select>` with our custom interactive search-enabled dropdown matching the mockup design in `CompanyDashboard.tsx`.
thinking:
**Pinpointing the Key Code**

I've just honed in on lines 2575-2587. It's the section handling the Active Company Selector, a `div` element. I see a `flex` layout, a hover effect, rounded corners, and padding. The classes suggest a subtle UI element, possibly with a border. It's a promising place to start examining the related functionality!


**Refining the UI Components**

I'm now diving deep into designing the new interactive dropdown component, focusing on pixel-perfect details based on the provided mockups. I've broken down the design into the pill button and the dropdown box, carefully considering the styling for each element. The pill button will use a rounded pill design with an icon, company name and chevron down, while the dropdown box will feature a search field, a divider, and a list of company items. I'm leveraging Tailwind classes for precise styling, ensuring consistency.

args.Description: "Replace company native select with a custom search dropdown matching mockup in CompanyDashboard.tsx"
args.Instruction: "Replace native select element with custom interactive search dropdown for companies in CompanyDashboard.tsx"
args.ReplacementContent:
" {/* Seletor de Empresa Ativa (Dropdown customizado estilo Mockup) */}
{isCompanyDropdownOpen && (
{/* Input de Busca */}
setCompanySearchQuery(e.target.value)} placeholder="Nome da empresa" className="bg-transparent border border-transparent outline-none font-semibold text-xs text-slate-700 p-0 w-full focus:ring-0 placeholder:text-slate-400 placeholder:font-normal" onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()} // Impede fechar ao clicar no campo />
args.toolAction: "Replacing native select with custom search dropdown markup in CompanyDashboard.tsx"
